refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: deriving, recording, and managing performance requirements. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these obligations are separated. This refined FR focuses solely on the derivation of performance requirements, which is a distinct functional need independent of how the requirements are recorded or managed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can analyze worksite mission context inputs and produce a documented set of performance requirements such as cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability without specifying how these are recorded or managed.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall record performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.
The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirements derived from the worksite mission context.

candidate_dps: Define a logical model to translate worksite mission context into performance metrics
Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets
Implement a version-controlled repository for performance baselines and calibration updates
Develop a ruleset to incorporate safety plans and operational policies into performance requirements
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a traceable mapping framework linking worksite inputs to performance targets

potential_sources: Tight coupling between safety plan rules and performance target derivation logic
Interdependency between calibration update handling and mission context interpretation
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a clear, traceable mapping which supports independence by separating input interpretation from update management and safety policy integration.

assumptions_risks: Assumes worksite inputs are well-defined and consistently formatted
Risk that changes in operational policies may require frequent updates to mapping framework
Maintaining traceability may increase documentation overhead
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating the handling of calibration updates and safety policy integration into distinct DPs or IMs to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for integrating worksite inputs and performance targets into a coherent traceable mapping framework.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data inputs from the worksite and sends processed performance metrics.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task assignments and performance targets to be linked with worksite inputs.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the mapping framework logically belongs to the overall solution integration; minimal diffusion as most data flows through this module.
Low containment since vehicle focuses on physical operations; mapping framework would require extensive data exchange outside vehicle.
Moderate containment but teleoperation mainly handles remote control, not data mapping; would cause diffusion.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety logic, unrelated to mapping framework.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the traceable mapping framework because it integrates diverse inputs and performance targets at the system level, ensuring high cohesion and minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution may lead to coupling with multiple interfaces if not well modularized internally.
If the mapping framework requires real-time vehicle data, interface latency or data consistency issues may arise.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly addresses the FR by establishing a traceable mapping framework that links worksite inputs to performance targets, fulfilling the requirement to derive performance requirements from the worksite mission context.

diffusion_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution may lead to coupling with multiple interfaces if not well modularized internally.
If the mapping framework requires real-time vehicle data, interface latency or data consistency issues may arise.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single IM boundary. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module interfaces with Telemetry and Tasking to receive inputs and produce traceable performance targets, which can be documented and audited against acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate is clearly identified as the NGHE Solution module, which encompasses the overall system responsible for integrating inputs and performance targets, providing clear allocation of the FR to the IM.

recommended_revisions: Consider internal modularization within the NGHE Solution module to manage complexity and minimize coupling with multiple interfaces.
Evaluate and mitigate potential latency or data consistency issues when integrating real-time vehicle data through interfaces.
Document the traceability and history maintenance processes explicitly within the NGHE Solution module to support verification.

decision.justification: Although the current FR to DP and DP to IM mappings have passed gates with clear traceability and ownership, the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks in the NGHE Solution module suggest that further decomposition of the FR is necessary. This will enable more granular control, verification, and mitigation of complexity and interface coupling risks. Decomposing into next-level sub-FRs will facilitate targeted design parameters and implementation modules that can better manage latency, data consistency, and modularization concerns.
next_level_sub_frs: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context
Map worksite inputs to performance targets
Maintain traceability and history of performance requirements

revise.why: To incorporate internal modularization within the NGHE Solution module to manage complexity and minimize coupling with multiple interfaces, and to explicitly document traceability and history maintenance processes supporting verification.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary implementation module responsible for integrating inputs and producing performance targets.
The current DP adequately addresses the FR at a high level but may conceal complexity and interface coupling risks.
Verification feasibility depends on clear traceability and documented processes within the IM.
open_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution leading to coupling with multiple interfaces if not well modularized.
Interface latency or data consistency issues when integrating real-time vehicle data.
Ensuring traceability and history maintenance processes are sufficiently documented for verification.
evidence_needed: Detailed internal architecture of NGHE Solution module showing modularization.
Latency and data consistency analysis for real-time vehicle data interfaces.
Verification plans and documentation for traceability and history maintenance.
